pages.text:
CHARCOAT IC SILICON RUBBER INSULATOR COATING
WORK METHOD STATEMENT
1. CLEANING
• On arrival at the loca(cid:415)on where the equipment must be coated, the bay in which the equipment insulators
are situated must be de-energised, isolated and made safe to work on by the appropriate earthing/grounding
methods.
• The surfaces to be coated must be cleaned of all contamina(cid:415)on before any further tasks are performed. For
loose dust/dirt, cleaning with water and a cloth should suffice. If the contamina(cid:415)on is more stubborn, then a
non-metallic domes(cid:415)c scouring pad and water should be employed. If there is a greasy residue then clean
ing/wiping the surface of the equipment with Toluene will be more than adequate.
• Should there be any deposits or marks that cannot be removed with the scouring pad, the area must be thor
oughly rinsed with water to leech out any conduc(cid:415)ve salts and then the primer and coa(cid:415)ng applied over the
top.
• Alterna(cid:415)vely, the use of high-pressure washers with clean water can be considered. Charcoat recommends a
nozzle that can expand/fan the water jet. The pressure to be used should be no more than 10 MPa and the
distance between nozzle and the insulator surface should be no less than 200mm in order not to harm the
equipment surface. The use of a Dry Ice High Pressure system can also be used.
2. BONDING AGENT APPLICATION
• Once clean and dry, the insulator is sprayed with CharCoat IC Primer, to ensure permanent adhesion of the
coa(cid:415)ng to the porcelain or glass surface.
• The bonding agent must be sprayed on as a very thin layer/fine mist using standard spray-pain(cid:415)ng equipment
with the spray gun nozzle having an aperture of 1.5mm. Ideal spraying pressure is 6.5 to 7 Bar (85 to 115 PSI).
The primer must just wet the surface without causing any run-off.
• A(cid:332)er applica(cid:415)on, the Primer must be allowed to become touch dry. This takes approximately 15 minutes. It
must be noted that the Primer must not be allowed to dry for more than 2 hours before applica(cid:415)on of the
Silicone Rubber Coa(cid:415)ng.
3. COATING MATERIAL PREPARATION
• A 1 or 5 litre can of the CharCoat IC Silicone rubber coa(cid:415)ng is to be opened and the small bo(cid:425)le of catalyst
provided with the material, added.
• The can is then to be re-closed and shaken for approximately two minutes. This ensures a uniform distribu(cid:415)on
of the filler materials and catalyst.
• It must be noted that the coa(cid:415)ng must be applied within 30 minutes of the addi(cid:415)on of the catalyst. Every (cid:415)me
that coa(cid:415)ng is decanted from the container, the sprayer must shake the container thoroughly again.
4. COATING APPLICATION
• The CharCoat IC Silicone Rubber Coa(cid:415)ng is to be applied with standard spray-pain(cid:415)ng equipment with the
spray gun nozzle having an aperture of 2mm and at an opera(cid:415)ng pressure of 6.5 to 7 Bar (85 to 115 PSI). The
coa(cid:415)ng technique should involve several smooth passes to build up a wet layer thickness of 0,3 to 0,5mm
without run-off.
• During spraying, the wet film thickness is to be checked with the Elcometer comb in at least three posi(cid:415)ons.
• The spraying area must be well ven(cid:415)lated and away from open flames. Personal Protec(cid:415)ve Equipment/cloth
ing and a respirator mask with appropriate filters must be worn.

pages.text:
5. CURING
• Following coa(cid:415)ng, the equipment surface will be touch dry within 20 minutes and the High Voltage equip
ment can be returned to service.
• The full cure of the Silicone Rubber coa(cid:415)ng will take between 48 and 72 hours a(cid:332)er applica(cid:415)on.
6. CLEANING OF SPRAY EQUIPMENT
• Empty the container of coa(cid:415)ng material and then pour 150ml of Toluene into the spray gun container,
assemble and shake, spray the Toluene into an empty coa(cid:415)ng container. The disassembled spray gun can
also be cleaned with Toluene.
